channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Bryan Kohberger trial: Idaho student murders survivor draws mask intruder wore on night of stabbings
item.description: An Idaho student murders survivor drew a sketch of the mask she says she saw on an intruder in her house the night four of her friends were killed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY county clerk refuses to file Texas' fine for doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills
item.description: A county clerk in New York refused Thursday to file a more than $100,000 judgment from Texas against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills to a woman near Dallas, setting up a potential challenge to laws designed to shield abortion providers who serve patients in states with abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Karen Read timeline: What to know ahead of her retrial
item.description: Jury selection will soon begin for the retrial of Karen Read, a Massachusetts woman accused of killing her police officer boyfriend in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors in the killings of 4 Idaho students seek to use man's crime scene paper as evidence
item.description: Prosecutors in the case of a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students are seeking to use as evidence a college paper he wrote that shows his knowledge of crime scenes, according to a court filing this week Bryan Kohberger, 30, is accused in the stabbing deaths of Ethan Chapin
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump shifts Greenland approach amid blowback, Signal scandal
item.description: Planned trips this week to Greenland’s capital, Nuuk, and a dogsledding race were canceled in favor of a more limited trip to visit American military installations on the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Anti-abortion groups aim to end Planned Parenthood funding and suggest Musk's DOGE to get it done
item.description: Major anti-abortion groups have gathered in the nation's capital to begin a lobbying effort with Congress and President Donald Trump’s administration aimed at eliminating funding for Planned Parenthood.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Million-dollar lottery ticket was nearly thrown out, winner says
item.description: “I was actually kind of going to throw them away, because I said ‘I’ve never won the lottery,’” said Joaquin Vaughn Redo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: SOMERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — A Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University has been detained by U.S. Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to court last month in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, according to court documents made public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging for the case to be moved so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Teens arrested after tractor-trailers struck by objects dropped from overpass
item.description: Two juvenile males, aged 16 and 17, have been charged with reckless endangerment after they reportedly threw multiple objects from an I-640 overpass.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, NewsNation

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked agents handcuffing her
item.description: Rumeysa Ozturk's whereabouts are unknown and no known charges have been filed against her, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Prominent political figures and lawmakers on the commencement speaker circuit are about to have some friendly, froggy competition.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-NFL player charged after 190 dogs seized from dog fighting venture
item.description: An Oklahoma man is facing federal charges after officials seized 190 dogs from an animal fighting venture, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Major department store to shutter some locations in California, other states
item.description: Kohl's announced 27 underperforming stores would close before April. The final day for those locations is coming this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Dollar Tree sells Family Dollar for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree has agreed to sell Family Dollar for $1 billion, ending the short-lived merger deal and allowing both companies to focus on their core operations.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KRON4

item.title: A new Chili’s near Scranton will be a throwback to 'The Office,' 'awesome blossom' and all
item.description: It has been nearly two decades since the workplace mockumentary "The Office” first set an episode inside a Chili's, where Michael Scott handed out Dundie awards to his ever-tolerant employees, including trophies for the whitest sneakers and for stinking up the bathroom.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KRON4

item.title: Vance to accompany wife to Greenland amid backlash
item.description: Vance’s announcement adds another layer of tension to an already fraught atmosphere surrounding his wife’s planned visit.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KRON4

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene fueled wildfires in North Carolina and South Carolina, where evacuation orders were in effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mia Love's family announces funeral plans
item.description: The family of Mia Love has announced funeral plans for the former Utah congresswoman, who passed away Sunday, March 23, at the age of 49, after battling brain cancer.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: LDS church applies for smaller temple in Fairview, Texas, after reaching agreement; new rendering released
item.description: The LDS church says it has filed a new application for a controversial temple in Fairview, Texas, after reaching an agreement with the town.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Video and 911 calls played by police detail chaos from deadly shooting in New Mexico
item.description: Police released video footage and 911 emergency calls Tuesday depicting chilling scenes of people running in panic as gunfire crackled in a New Mexico park where three people were killed and 15 others were wounded during an unauthorized car show.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mexican cartel member stockpiled ammo on border
item.description: A Mexican-national member of the Cartel Del Noreste has been sentenced to five years in prison for illegally possessing ammunition on the South Texas border, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Chick-fil-A unveils new item you can't find in restaurants
item.description: Chick-fil-A is rolling out a new bottled dressing that fans won’t find at any of its 3,100 U.S. restaurants.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: US infrastructure improved with Biden-era spending but there's a long way to go
item.description: A once-every-four-years report card on the upkeep of America's infrastructure gave it a “C” grade on Tuesday, up slightly from previous reports, largely due to investments made during former President Joe Biden's administration.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USC star JuJu Watkins suffers season-ending knee injury in March Madness win over Mississippi State
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — JuJu Watkins was carried off the floor with a season-ending knee injury in the first quarter of top-seeded Southern California’s game against Mississippi State in the NCAA Tournament on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy resigns after 5 years in the position
item.description: The head of the beleaguered U.S. Postal Service, Louis DeJoy, resigned Monday after nearly five years in the position, leaving as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency have floated the idea of privatizing mail service.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deadly hit-and-run in New Mexico brings juvenile justice challenges into focus
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Behind the wheel of the stolen car was a 12-year-old boy. In the front passenger seat was a boy who just days earlier had marked his 11th birthday.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks three agencies from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday indefinitely blocked the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies, the latest roadblock to its efforts to slim down the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Tax season is a prime time for scams. IRS uncertainty could add to the issues this year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a lot of information (and money) on the table during tax season. That also makes it a prime time for scams. Year-round, fraudsters may use a handful of common tactics to try to steal your identity, money or other sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4
